---
title: "P1 --- Clean prediction-window mortality model on PIC v1.1.0"
author: "[PhD student], Max Moldovan, Usman Iqbal"
date: "2026-05-08"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: true
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{P1 --- clean prediction-window baseline}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

> **Subtitle.** Manuscript draft (vignette form). Authors: PhD student (Bond, lead), Max Moldovan (Adelaide, ORCID 0000-0001-9680-8474), Usman Iqbal (Bond, senior).
>
> **Abstract.** *To be drafted at gates G4–G7.* Headline framing: calibration + decision-curve analysis on PIM3 vs penalised logistic regression vs XGBoost, with the prediction-window leakage defects of the prior literature explicitly fixed.

# Status

Draft scaffold. Sections populated progressively as gates land:

- **G1 cohort** → §Methods.Cohort, §Results.Attrition
- **G2 features + leakage audit** → §Methods.Features
- **G3 PIM3** → §Methods.Baseline, §Results.PIM3
- **G4 fits + calibration** → §Results.Calibration (headline)
- **G5 DCA** → §Results.Decision-curve (headline)
- **G6 discrimination + subgroups** → §Results.Discrimination
- **G7 TRIPOD-AI compliance** → §Methods, §Supplementary
- **G8 reproducibility** → §Methods.Reproducibility

The cohort specification is inherited verbatim from `cohort_spec.Rmd`.
This vignette does not redefine the cohort.

# Background

[To be written at G1. Frame: paediatric ICU mortality prediction; LOS
leakage in the prior literature; PIM3 as the principled comparator;
calibration + DCA as the headline framing.]

# Methods

## Cohort

See `vignettes/cohort_spec.Rmd`. Brief recap in the manuscript.

## Features

See `R/features.R`. T<sub>0</sub> + 24 h lock; `audit_no_leakage()` enforced.

## Baseline (PIM3)

See `R/pim3.R`.

## Models

Penalised logistic regression (elastic net) and XGBoost. Hyperparameter
search via inner 5-fold CV; canonical small grids documented in the
package vignette and frozen at study start.

## Evaluation

Calibration suite (slope, intercept, ICI, calibration-in-the-large) +
decision-curve analysis at 5%, 10%, 20% thresholds as the headline.
AUROC, AUPRC, Brier, Brier skill score reported as supporting.

## Reproducibility

`targets::tar_make()` from raw CSVs to manuscript figures.

# Results

[Populated G1–G6.]

# Discussion

[Drafted G7.]

# TRIPOD-AI checklist

[Item-by-item; supplementary appendix.]
